hypothesis: Incorporating a Dirichlet-process-based noise schedule into the forward diffusion process—replacing the standard Gaussian noise with noise sampled from a Dirichlet distribution that naturally respects the simplex geometry of compositional data—will simultaneously eliminate the need for explicit non-negativity and sum-to-one regularization penalties while achieving higher validity, density, and coverage than PCDiff's penalty-based approach, because the intrinsic support of the Dirichlet distribution enforces physical constraints by construction throughout every denoising step rather than as a soft post-hoc correction.
